layman_headline: Market fear is high but no clear safe entry exists — staying patient.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in a fearful state with Bitcoin dominance squeezing altcoins. While several coins have dropped significantly, none of them present a clean technical setup with confirmed reversal signals. Most candidates are in downtrends below their key moving averages, and the ones that have bounced recently may already be extended. The smart move right now is to wait for a better entry rather than chase weak setups.
layman_action: Do nothing this cycle. Keep cash ready. If Ethereum pulls back to the $1,800-$1,810 range and holds that support with volume, that could become the next entry. Watch for BTC to break above $65K convincingly as a green light for alts.
expert_reasoning: All swing candidates present unfavorable setups. BONK has a strongly bearish chart (-2.0 bias) with Three Black Crows and Bearish Marubozu — a classic falling knife. PUMP is up 40% in 7 days and likely overextended. NIGHT has no chart data at all — pure speculation. 币安人生 has a bull flag but RSI at 58 and poor R/R (0.97x). ETH is the most attractive on a pullback basis: RSI at 39 (our highest-confidence signal type at 80% win rate), massive liquidity, and the Blockworks headline about 'ETH derivatives reset' suggests positioning is being cleaned out. However, the chart bias is -0.70 with price below all MAs, so I'm waiting for a test and hold of $1,800 support before committing capital. BTC dominance at 56.6% is hostile to alts, and Fear & Greed at 29 is improving but not yet at the extreme fear levels (sub-20) that historically mark reliable bottoms.
risk_factors: BTC is hitting resistance at $65K — rejection here could drag all alts down sharply
BTC dominance at 56.6% means capital is flowing away from altcoins into Bitcoin
Fear & Greed at 29 is improving but could easily reverse if macro headlines worsen
watch_list: BTC price action at $65,000 resistance — breakout vs rejection determines alt direction
ETH holding $1,800 support level on any pullback with volume confirmation
Fear & Greed index — a drop below 20 would signal extreme fear and better contrarian entries
market_context: Bitcoin is testing $65K resistance amid institutional tech sell-offs, while BTC dominance at 56.6% continues to suppress altcoin performance. Fear & Greed is at 29 and improving from 26 last week, but the broader environment remains risk-off for alts.
context_banner: High BTC dominance and fearful sentiment make this a patience cycle — capital preservation beats forced entries.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 39 is approaching oversold territory — our most reliable signal type (80% win rate) — but not yet at the <30 level that would trigger a high-conviction entry.
Fear & Greed at 29 (Fear) improving from 26 weekly average. Trend is positive but not yet at extreme fear levels that historically produce the best contrarian entries.
Bitcoin hitting $65K wall amid institutional tech sell-off suggests near-term resistance that could cause another leg down in alts before any breakout.
ETH derivatives reset headline suggests leveraged positioning is being flushed — a precondition for healthier price discovery and potential reversal.
BONK chart at -2.0 bias with Three Black Crows confirms falling knife — avoided. Most candidates below all MAs with bearish chart structures.
